response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and has built low-latency, high-throughput systems in DLang at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, which demonstrates systems-level thinking close to what this role needs. However, the core requirement — modern C++ or Rust in commercial settings — is weakly met: the only professional C++ experience was a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago, and recent work has centered on TypeScript/Node.js product engineering. There is also no direct routing or navigation-guidance experience. Worth a conversation only if the team is open to ramping a strong systems generalist onto C++.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS applications
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at ad-tech scale
Broad full-stack versatility with strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience — last professional use was ~2015, no Rust
No routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance systems background
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node product work, far from data-pipeline systems engineering
